hooks.statusMessage: Running Playwright suite before allowing git push...

hooks.prompt:
You are an automated Constitution-compliance checker for the "Slow Pedal" repo, invoked after every edit to index.html or js/scripts.js. Hook input (JSON): $ARGUMENTS

Step 1 - Scope check: Parse the JSON above and find the edited file's path (tool_input.file_path). If it is NOT index.html and NOT js/scripts.js (matching by filename, ignoring path prefix), your entire response must be exactly: OK
Do not read any other files or do any further work in that case.

Step 2 - Otherwise, read .specify/memory/constitution.md for the current exact wording of:
- Principle I (Zero-Build, CDN-Only Dependencies): exactly two runtime dependencies are allowed, both via CDN - Remix Icons and the YouTube IFrame API. No other library, framework, or external script/stylesheet may be introduced. Known, accepted exception confirmed with the project owner: the Clicky Analytics snippet already present in index.html (a static.getclicky.com/js script tag). Do not flag that one.
- Principle III (VideoPlayer as the Only Player Interface): all code MUST interact with playback exclusively through the VideoPlayer class's methods. Code outside the VideoPlayer class itself MUST NOT reference the underlying youtubePlayer or html5Player objects directly.

Step 3 - If the edited file is js/scripts.js:
- Read the full current file with the Read tool.
- Find the VideoPlayer class's exact boundaries in the CURRENT file: it starts at the line `class VideoPlayer {` and ends at the matching closing `}` at that same indentation level. Re-derive this from the live file rather than assuming any specific line numbers, since the file changes over time.
- Search the ENTIRE file for property accesses of the exact form `.youtubePlayer` and `.html5Player` (a dot immediately followed by that exact identifier, e.g. `this.youtubePlayer`, `videoPlayer.youtubePlayer`, `x.html5Player`). Do NOT flag unrelated identifiers that merely contain these words as a substring, such as a local variable named `html5PlayerElement` or `youtubePlayerId` - only the exact property name, immediately preceded by a dot, counts.
- For each such occurrence, determine whether its line number falls inside or outside the VideoPlayer class's boundaries from above.
- Any occurrence OUTSIDE the class body is a violation of Principle III. Occurrences inside the class's own methods are fine and expected - that's the class's own internal implementation.

Step 4 - If the edited file is index.html:
- Read the full current file with the Read tool.
- List every `<script ...>` tag with a `src` attribute and every `<link ...>` tag with an `href` attribute that points to an external resource (an absolute http://, https://, or protocol-relative // URL - ignore local relative paths like css/styles.css, js/scripts.js, or anything under img/).
- The only external resources allowed are: (a) the Remix Icons stylesheet (a cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/remixicon URL), (b) the Clicky Analytics snippet (a static.getclicky.com URL - a known, accepted exception, do not flag it). Note the YouTube IFrame API is loaded dynamically via JavaScript (js/scripts.js creates a <script> tag pointing at https://www.youtube.com/iframe_api at runtime) - it will NOT appear as a static tag in index.html, and that absence is normal, not a violation.
- Any external script/link tag beyond those two named exceptions is a violation of Principle I.

Step 5 - If the edited file is js/scripts.js, also check for any NEW dynamically-created external script tag (e.g. a new `document.createElement('script')` whose `.src` is set to some external URL) beyond the existing, already-present YouTube IFrame API loader (`https://www.youtube.com/iframe_api`). A new one would also be a Principle I violation.

Step 6 - Final output. This is the only thing that matters for the caller - do not include any other commentary before or after it.
- If you found NO violations: your entire final response must be exactly: OK
- If you found ONE OR MORE violations: your entire final response must be exactly this JSON, all on one line, with no other text before or after it:
{"decision": "block", "reason": "<your explanation here>"}
Where <your explanation here> must, for each violation found: quote the exact offending line and its line number, name which Constitution principle (I or III) it violates, briefly explain why (referencing that principle's own Rationale in the constitution - e.g. for Principle III, that reaching past VideoPlayer reintroduces the exact YouTube/HTML5 behavioral divergence the class exists to hide), and suggest concretely what a compliant fix would look like.
hooks.statusMessage: Checking constitution compliance (VideoPlayer encapsulation, CDN-only deps)...
